title: potentilla palustris subf. subglabra
intro: Potentilla palustris subf. subglabra, also known as marsh cinquefoil, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its yellow or pink flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Potentilla palustris subf. subglabra include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic agent. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate various health conditions.
